title: Anki / spaced-repetition deck exporter
context: Anki is a widely used open-source spaced-repetition flashcard system. Single-select MCQ and true/false items project naturally to Anki Basic or Cloze card types; the stem + correct answer + rationale maps well to front/back format. This exporter overlaps with the open-flashcards project (which may share the canonical model's stem/choice/rationale structure); coordination is required to avoid duplication.
objective: Implement an Anki .apkg deck exporter that projects canonical bank items (single-select MCQ, true/false, short-text) to Anki Basic or Cloze card format.
acceptanceCriteria: Projects single-select MCQ, true/false, and short-text items to Anki Basic or Cloze cards; stem → front, answer + rationale → back.
Unsupported item types (matching, ordering, multi-select) gracefully excluded from Anki output with a log message.
Output is a valid .apkg file importable into Anki Desktop or AnkiWeb.
Golden fixtures and CI tests provided.
Coordination note with open-flashcards project documented to avoid duplication.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: Anki .apkg format documentation and genanki Python library (or equivalent Node library)
output: A CI-integrated Anki .apkg exporter (MIT-licensed Node package) with golden fixtures and verified import round-trip.
